mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated monitoring and scaling capabilities. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM Cloud infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the IBM Cloud CLI, which is necessary to interact with IBM Cloud services.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the IBM Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --kube-version 1.30 --num-workers 2

```

> Change 'my-cluster' to your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This sets the context for kubectl to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step enables autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --enable-autoscale

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --enable-monitoring

```

> Enables autoscaling for your cluster.
> Enables monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> Use --force to skip confirmation.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This command ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-delete my-cluster --force

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This command upgrades the cluster to the latest version.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.30

```

> Ensure your cluster is compatible with the new version.

upgrade.title: Verify upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the nodes are still in a 'Ready' state.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check that all nodes are 'Ready' after the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Rollback if necessary
upgrade.description:
If issues arise, you can rollback to the previous version using the following command.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.29

```

> Replace with the previous version if needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient quota'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Your IBM Cloud account may have reached its resource quota limits.

**Fix:**
Check your resource quotas in the IBM Cloud dashboard and request an increase if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster creation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to invalid parameters or insufficient resources in the selected zone.

**Fix:**
Verify the parameters used in the cluster creation command and ensure the selected zone has available resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl && chmod +x ./kubectl && sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Your IBM Cloud CLI session may have expired or you may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Re-authenticate using 'ibmcloud login' and ensure you have the correct permissions for the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30 features including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure that your applications are compatible with the new scheduling features.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed before proceeding.
